architecture.summary: The paper proposes Tensor Functional Language Logic (T-FLL), a formal framework that maps linguistic units to functional noemes and logical operators to tensor operations. Sentences are converted into noematic formulae, which are claimed to direct the attention mechanism more efficiently than standard statistical token prediction. The system bridges symbolic reasoning and continuous neural manifolds by redefining how LLMs process semantic content.

biblio.title: Tensor Logic of Embedding Vectors in Neural Networks

claims.statement: Current Artificial Neural Networks based on Large Language Models primarily use statistical token prediction, often lacking rigorous structural semantic consistency and illocutionary force
The attention mechanism driving the semantics of a dialog can be reformulated more efficiently if directed by the noematic formulae
Tensor Functional Language Logic (T-FLL) provides a formal bridge between symbolic reasoning and continuous neural manifolds
Logical operators can be mapped onto tensor operations within the proposed framework
